description: For each cluster pair, find the two theorems (one per cluster) with the lowest normalized Levenshtein edit distance on normalized tactic sequences (top-30 clusters, longest proofs, >=5 tactics); report pairs below the within-cluster baseline.
motivation: Probe whether cross-domain bridges can be found via shared proof STRUCTURE (similar tactic sequences) rather than shared premises - to catch conceptual bridges.
formal_results: 200 cross-cluster pairs; closest normalized edit distance 0.586 (within-cluster median ~1.000). LinearAlgebra appears in 20 of the top-30 cross-cluster pairs (a lingua franca), then Analysis, RingTheory.
results_description: Even the most similar cross-domain proofs differ substantially (ED ~0.59); structural similarity across domains is modest.
conclusions: A second, CONCEPTUAL class of bridge exists - pairs like Brouwer<->Lawvere (shared diagonal argument) and Lie algebras<->Lie groups (tangent-space correspondence) share proof STRUCTURE, not premises, so the premise-based detector misses them. Closing the operational-vs-conceptual gap is the central open problem.
